'''Vila Matautia''' is a Samoan former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s.<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He was a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] international and played at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]]. He also played with St Helens in the [[Super League]], having championship and [[Challenge Cup]] Final success with them.

==Background==
Matautia was born in [[Samoa]].

==Club career==
Matautia started his career at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in 1990.<ref>{{cite web|title=Player Statistics|url=http://www.robterrace.com/dons/player.php?PID=622|publisher=R.Terrace, R.Green and Doncaster RLFC|access-date=2 January 2018}}</ref>

Matautia played for St Helens from the interchange bench in their [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final victory over [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|access-date=3 June 2012|archive-date=8 August 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref>
Matautia played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] from the interchange bench in their [[1999 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Bradford Bulls]].

Matautia appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] (replacing {{rlp|PR}} [[Ian Pickavance]] on 25-minutes) in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]' 16-25 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1995–96 League Cup (rugby league)|1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref name="13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-date=7 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|url-status=dead}}</ref> As [[Super League V]] champions, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] played against [[2000 NRL season|2000 NRL]] Premiers, the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2001 World Club Challenge]]. Matautia played from the interchange bench in Saints' victory.

Matautia left Saints and joined [[Leigh Centurions]] in 2002.<ref>{{cite web|title=They Played For Leigh|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/they-played-for-leigh|website=Leigh Centurions|access-date=2 January 2018}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Matautia represented [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]], and played at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]]. He also played for the United States rugby league team, trying to get them into the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]] through his grandmother who was born in Hawaii.<ref>{{cite web|last = Hadfield
|first = Dave
|title = Lebanese have talent to star on the Disney stage
|work = [[The Independent]]
|publisher = independent.co.uk
|date = 1999-11-21
|url = https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/lebanese-have-talent-to-star-on-the-disney-stage-741481.html
|access-date = 2009-10-06}}</ref>

==Assault conviction==
In December 2017, he assaulted a 15-year-old [[Rainford High Technology College]] pupil, inflicting a broken nose, fractured cheek bone and severe bruising. In July 2018, he pleaded guilty and received a seven-month sentence, suspended for 18 months. He received this without hearing victim impact statement and hours before police and victims family attended sentencing. He also received a 3 year restraining order to not contact in any form the victim and his family, which run up to the trial he had tried to do.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.liverpoolecho.co.uk/news/liverpool-news/former-top-rugby-player-knocked-14892176|title = Former top rugby player knocked child, 15, unconscious for 'bullying his son'|date = 10 July 2018}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/news/16368491.warning-graphic-images-horrific-injuries-caused-to-rainford-pupil-by-ex-saints-star-vila-matautia/|title = WARNING GRAPHIC IMAGES: 'Horrific' injuries caused to Rainford pupil by ex-Saints star Vila Matautia| date=20 July 2018 }}</ref>
